children.heading: .—Existence
children.label: a .—Existence
children.text: There is in the Treasury a fund known as the Federal Buildings Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: User charges under , payable in advance or otherwise.section 586(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Proceeds from the lease of federal building sites or additions under .section 581(d) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Receipts from carriers and others for loss of, or damage to, property belonging to the Fund.

children.heading: .—Reimbursements for special services
children.label: 2 .—Reimbursements for special services
children.text: This subchapter does not preclude the Administrator of General Services from providing special services, not included in the standard level user charge, on a reimbursable basis. The reimbursements may be credited to the Fund.

children.heading: .—Transfer of surplus amounts
children.label: 3 .—Transfer of surplus amounts
children.text: To prevent the accumulation of excessive surpluses in the Fund, in any fiscal year an amount specified in an appropriation law may be transferred out of the Fund and deposited as miscellaneous receipts in the Treasury.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Deposits in the Fund are available for real property management and related activities in the amounts specified in annual appropriation laws without regard to fiscal year limitations.

children.heading: .—Salaries and expenses related to construction projects or planning programs
children.label: 2 .—Salaries and expenses related to construction projects or planning programs
children.text: Deposits in the Fund that are available pursuant to annual appropriation laws may be transferred and consolidated on the books of the Treasury into a special account in accordance with, and for the purposes specified in, .section 3176 of this title

children.heading: .—Repayment of general services administration borrowing from federal financing bank
children.label: 3 .—Repayment of general services administration borrowing from federal financing bank
children.text: The Administrator, in accordance with rules and procedures that the Office of Management and Budget and the Secretary of the Treasury establish, may transfer from the Fund an amount necessary to repay the principal amount of a General Services Administration borrowing from the Federal Financing Bank, if the borrowing is a legal obligation of the Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: A building constructed pursuant to the purchase contract authority of section 5 of the Public Buildings Amendments of 1972 (, ).Public Law 92–31386 Stat. 219

children.label: B
children.text: A building occupied pursuant to an installment purchase contract.

children.label: C
children.text: A building under the control of a department or agency, if alterations of the building are required in connection with moving the department or agency from a former building that is, or will be, under the control of the Administration.

children.heading: .—Receiving cash incentives
children.label: 1 .—Receiving cash incentives
children.text: The Administrator may receive amounts from rebates or other cash incentives related to energy savings and shall deposit the amounts in the Fund for use as provided in paragraph (4).

children.heading: .—Receiving goods or services
children.label: 2 .—Receiving goods or services
children.text: The Administrator may accept, from a utility, goods or services that enhance the energy efficiency of federal facilities.

children.heading: .—Assignment of energy rebates
children.label: 3 .—Assignment of energy rebates
children.text: In the administration of real property that the Administrator leases and for which the Administrator pays utility costs, the Administrator may assign all or a portion of energy rebates to the lessor to underwrite the costs incurred in undertaking energy efficiency improvements in the real property if the payback period for the improvement is at least 2 years less than the remainder of the term of the lease.

children.heading: .—Obligating amounts for energy management improvement programs
children.label: 4 .—Obligating amounts for energy management improvement programs
A
children.text: amounts received and deposited in the Fund under paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: goods and services received under paragraph (2); and

children.label: C
children.text: amounts the Administrator determines are not needed for other authorized projects and that are otherwise available to implement energy efficiency programs.

children.heading: .—Receiving amounts
children.label: 1 .—Receiving amounts
children.text: The Administrator may receive amounts from the sale of recycled materials and shall deposit the amounts in the Fund for use as provided in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: promote further source reduction and recycling programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: encourage employees to participate in recycling programs by providing financing for child care.

children.heading: .—Additional Authority Related to Energy Management and Recycling Programs
children.label: f .—Additional Authority Related to Energy Management and Recycling Programs
children.text: The Fund may receive, in the form of rebates, cash incentives or otherwise, any revenues, collections, or other income related to energy savings or recycling efforts. Amounts received under this subsection remain in the Fund until expended and remain available for federal energy management improvement programs, recycling programs, or employee programs that are authorized by law or that the Administrator considers appropriate. The Administration may use amounts received under this subsection, in addition to amounts received as New Obligational Authority, in activities of the Fund as necessary.
